questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Former mining regions are being revitalised through:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The furniture (A) / in this room (B) / are very (C) / expensive. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Each of (A) / the participants (B) / were given (C) / a certificate. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

According to the passage, India has the ____ largest coal reserves in the world.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / more smarter (B) / than his (C) / elder brother. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The Coal Mines Regulations were enacted in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The team (A) / are working (B) / on the new (C) / project since May. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Coal grading is primarily based on:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither he (A) / nor his brother (B) / are going (C) / to the party. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The word 'legacy' in the passage most nearly means:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I am looking (A) / forward to (B) / meet you (C) / next week. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

What percentage of India's coal output does CIL account for?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / the tallest (B) / of the two (C) / sisters. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The government's renewable energy target by 2030 is ____ GW.

questions.stem: Find the error: The scenery (A) / of the mountains (B) / are (C) / breathtaking. (D)

questions.stem: Meaning of 'To face the music':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of MUNDANE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The internet has revolutionised communication. Q: People can now connect instantly across the globe. R: Social media platforms enable sharing of information. S: However, digital divide remains a concern.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The organisation ____ to improve its bottom line.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The furniture in this room are expensive.
options: The furniture in this room are expensive.
The furnitures in this room are expensive.
The furniture in this room is expensive.
The furniture in this room were expensive.
questions.solution: The furniture in this room is expensive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The mine was ____ after the accident.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
options: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EXUBERANT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of LETHARGIC:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A downward (trough-shaped) fold, with the youngest beds in its core, is a:
questions.solution: A syncline sags downward; limbs dip toward the axis and youngest rocks lie in the core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The chemical composition of quartz is:
questions.solution: Quartz is silicon dioxide, SiO₂, one of the commonest minerals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition states that, in an undisturbed sequence, the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: Sediments are deposited oldest-first, so the lowest undisturbed bed is the oldest.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 20000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 20000×30 = 600000 m³ = 0.6 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good index (guide) fossil should be:
options: Widespread but short-lived in geologic time
questions.solution: Wide distribution plus a short time range makes a fossil useful for correlation/dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Earth's outer core is inferred to be liquid because:
options: Both P- and S-waves speed up there
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves cannot travel through liquids, creating an S-wave shadow zone — evidence of a liquid outer core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 6 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which pairing of fault type and stress is correct?
questions.solution: Tension produces normal faults; compression produces reverse/thrust faults.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 30 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principle of original horizontality states that sediments are originally deposited:
questions.solution: Tilted/folded strata indicate later deformation, since beds start horizontal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The theory that the Earth's lithosphere is divided into moving plates is:
questions.solution: Plate tectonics explains earthquakes, volcanism and mountain building by plate interactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 45°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 45°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 45°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Walther's law states that facies occurring in a conformable vertical succession also occur:
options: Laterally adjacent in the original depositional environments
questions.solution: Conformably superposed facies represent environments that were originally side by side and migrated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 45° shows a width of 10 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A buried erosion surface separating older from much younger rocks is a(n):
questions.solution: An unconformity represents a gap (missing time) in the rock record.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The boundary separating the crust from the mantle is the:
questions.solution: The Moho marks the crust–mantle boundary, identified by a jump in seismic velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral's tendency to break along smooth planes related to its atomic structure is its:
questions.solution: Cleavage reflects planes of weak bonding in the crystal lattice.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 5% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Granite cools faster than basalt, giving it finer grains
questions.solution: Granite cools slowly at depth (coarse grains); basalt cools fast at the surface (fine grains) — the claim is reversed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with surface (opencast) mining, underground coal mining is generally chosen when the seam is:
options: Deep, so that the stripping ratio becomes uneconomic
questions.solution: When overburden-to-coal (stripping) ratio is too high for opencast, underground methods become economic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 10 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity and magnetic surveys are examples of ____ geophysical methods:
questions.solution: Gravity and magnetics map potential fields arising from density and susceptibility contrasts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved down relative to the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall down = normal fault, caused by extension/tension.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="70" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="90" x2="245" y2="90" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="82" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: normal fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bowen's reaction series predicts that the first silicate mineral to crystallize from a cooling basaltic magma is:
questions.solution: In the discontinuous series olivine crystallizes first at the highest temperature; quartz last.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The angle that a bed makes with the horizontal, measured perpendicular to strike, is the:
questions.solution: Dip is the maximum inclination of a plane; strike is the horizontal line on it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 10 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral that effervesces (fizzes) when dilute hydrochloric acid is applied is:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO₃) reacts with dilute HCl, releasing CO₂ — a standard field test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 80 m on flat ground and dips at 35°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 80×sin35° ≈ 45.89 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: From the surface inward, the major layers of the Earth are:
questions.solution: The Earth is layered into a thin crust, a thick mantle, and a metallic core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about porosity and permeability is correct?
options: Permeability is independent of pore connectivity
Sand has lower porosity than shale always
Clay can have high porosity but low permeability
High porosity always means high permeability
questions.solution: Clay's tiny, poorly connected pores hold much water yet transmit it slowly — high porosity, low permeability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the undisturbed stratigraphic column shown (top to base: A, B, C, D), the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: By the law of superposition, the lowest bed (D) is the oldest in an undisturbed sequence.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 180 150" width="180" height="150"><rect x="40" y="15" width="100" height="28" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="33" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">A</text><rect x="40" y="43" width="100" height="28" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">B</text><rect x="40" y="71" width="100" height="28" fill="#bbf7d0" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="89" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">C</text><rect x="40" y="99" width="100" height="28" fill="#fecaca" stroke="#475569"/><text x="90" y="117" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">D</text><text x="150" y="33" font-size="9" fill="#475569">top</text><text x="150" y="117" font-size="9" fill="#475569">base</text></svg>
figure.caption: Stratigraphic column

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coal rank with the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value is:
questions.solution: Anthracite is the highest-rank coal, with the greatest carbon content and heating value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 78 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Mohs scale of hardness, the hardest mineral (value 10) is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale runs from talc (1) to diamond (10), the hardest natural mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fastest seismic body waves, which arrive first at a station, are:
questions.solution: P-waves are compressional and travel fastest, arriving before slower S and surface waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds sag downward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Downward-sagging limbs dipping toward the axis define a syncline (youngest beds in core).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><path d="M20 30 Q 130 100 240 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M20 55 Q 130 120 240 55" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="130" y1="15" x2="130" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="130" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">axial plane</text></svg>
figure.caption: syncline (cross-section)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Mohs hardness, which comparison is correct?
questions.solution: A harder mineral scratches a softer one; quartz (7) > feldspar (6).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 7 km/s and arrives 30 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about coal ranks is INCORRECT?
options: Lignite has a higher carbon content than anthracite
questions.solution: Anthracite, not lignite, has the highest carbon content — the statement is reversed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sandstone is a:
options: Clastic sedimentary rock made of sand-sized grains
questions.solution: Sandstone is a clastic rock of cemented sand (mostly quartz) grains.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 30°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 30°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 30°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A reverse (or thrust) fault is produced mainly by:
questions.solution: Compression pushes the hanging wall up over the footwall — a reverse/thrust fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 12.5% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: We currently live in which geological era?
questions.solution: The Cenozoic ('age of mammals') is the present era, following the Mesozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz (hardness 7) will scratch, but not be scratched by, which mineral?
questions.solution: A mineral scratches anything softer; quartz (7) scratches feldspar (6) but not topaz/corundum/diamond.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 2 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 2×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 5.6 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heavy minerals concentrated by flowing water in stream beds form a:
questions.solution: Placers (e.g. of gold, cassiterite) form where moving water sorts dense, durable grains.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 50% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity of a rock refers to its:
questions.solution: Porosity is the percentage of void space; permeability is the ability to transmit fluid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The colour of a mineral's powder, seen when it is rubbed on a porcelain plate, is its:
questions.solution: Streak is the colour of the fine powder, often more diagnostic than the surface colour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary rocks are formed mainly by:
questions.solution: Weathered material is transported, deposited, compacted and cemented into sedimentary rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds arch upward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Upward-arching limbs dipping away from the axis define an anticline (oldest beds in core).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><path d="M20 90 Q 130 20 240 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M20 110 Q 130 45 240 110" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="130" y1="15" x2="130" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="130" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">axial plane</text></svg>
figure.caption: anticline (cross-section)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Basalt is a(n):
questions.solution: Basalt is the fine-grained, dark, mafic rock formed from rapidly cooled lava.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an ore, the valueless minerals associated with the ore mineral are called:
questions.solution: Gangue is the commercially worthless material that must be separated from the ore mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 20°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 20°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 20°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The law of superposition reliably gives relative age only when the strata are:
questions.solution: Tectonic overturning can invert beds, so superposition needs an undisturbed (right-way-up) sequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 5 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 5 Mt × 3% = 150 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The upper surface of the zone of saturation in the ground is the:
questions.solution: The water table marks the top of the saturated zone, below which pores are water-filled.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about anticlines is correct?
options: The youngest beds lie in the core of an anticline
An anticline is a downward fold
The oldest beds lie in the core of an anticline
questions.solution: Erosion of an upfold exposes the oldest strata at its core; anticlines are classic hydrocarbon traps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 60°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 60°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 60°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A volcanic rock containing many gas-bubble cavities is described as:
questions.solution: Vesicles are voids left by escaping gas in rapidly cooled lava (e.g. scoria, pumice).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 20 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 4 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arranged from lowest to highest rank, the coal series is:
questions.solution: Increasing rank (carbon content) goes peat, lignite, sub-bituminous/bituminous, anthracite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A normal fault is produced mainly by:
questions.solution: Tension drops the hanging wall down relative to the footwall — a normal fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 54 g has a volume of 20 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement comparing seismic waves is correct?
options: S-waves cannot travel through liquids, but P-waves can
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves need a solid; compressional (P) waves pass through solids, liquids and gases, and are faster.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 200 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The progressive transformation of plant matter into higher-rank coal is called:
questions.solution: Coalification increases carbon and reduces moisture/volatiles as burial heat and pressure rise.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a plunging anticline eroded to a level surface, the outcrop pattern of beds forms a:
options: V or nose closing in the direction of plunge
questions.solution: Plunging folds produce V-shaped (zig-zag) outcrops; an anticline's nose points in the plunge direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 3 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 20 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 3 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant single mineral group in the crust is:
questions.solution: Feldspars make up roughly half of the Earth's crust by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 1 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 1 Mt × 3% = 30 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fracture in rock along which there is no appreciable displacement is a:
questions.solution: Joints are cracks without relative movement of the blocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Most of India's coal occurs in rocks of which geological system?
questions.solution: The bulk of Indian coal lies in Lower Gondwana (Permian) sediments, e.g. the Barakar Formation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using a simplified proxy where calorific value ≈ 70 × fixed-carbon%, a coal with 40% fixed carbon has a calorific value of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite is best classified as a(n):
questions.solution: Granite cools slowly at depth, producing large crystals; it is felsic and intrusive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 1 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the proximate analysis of coal, fixed carbon is obtained by subtracting from 100% the sum of:
questions.solution: Fixed carbon (%) = 100 − (moisture + volatile matter + ash) in proximate analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As coal rank increases from lignite to anthracite, the volatile matter and moisture generally:
questions.solution: Higher rank means more fixed carbon and less moisture/volatiles, raising calorific value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Index minerals such as chlorite → biotite → garnet → kyanite → sillimanite mark increasing:
questions.solution: These index minerals appear at successive isograds, recording rising metamorphic grade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 10 m over a horizontal distance of 40 m. Its dip angle is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fracture in rock along which there has been observable displacement is a:
questions.solution: Faults show relative movement of the two blocks; joints show none.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant group of rock-forming minerals in the Earth's crust is the:
questions.solution: Silicate minerals (feldspar, quartz, mica, etc.) dominate the crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mica is well known for its perfect:
options: Basal cleavage (it splits into thin sheets)
questions.solution: Mica has one perfect cleavage direction, peeling into thin elastic sheets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 30 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 30 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 4 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A porphyritic texture (large crystals in a fine groundmass) indicates:
options: Two stages of cooling — slow then fast
questions.solution: Early slow cooling grows phenocrysts; later rapid cooling forms the fine matrix.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that groundwater discharge is proportional to the hydraulic gradient and the:
questions.solution: Q = K·i·A; flow rises with hydraulic conductivity K and gradient i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved up over the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall up = reverse fault, caused by compression.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="40" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="60" x2="245" y2="60" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="52" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: reverse fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The in-place breakdown of rock by mechanical and chemical processes is:
questions.solution: Weathering disintegrates/decomposes rock in situ; erosion then removes the products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.6. The mineable reserve is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude of an earthquake is commonly expressed on the:
questions.solution: Earthquake size is reported as magnitude (Richter / moment magnitude).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock body that can store and transmit usable amounts of groundwater is a(n):
questions.solution: An aquifer is porous and permeable enough to yield water to wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 2 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 4.5 (in the given time units). Its age is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Igneous rocks are formed by the:
options: Cooling and solidification of magma or lava
questions.solution: Igneous rocks crystallize from molten material (magma below ground, lava above).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 15 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
